seo.title: Veo 3.1 Fast | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Veo 3.1 Fast on MaxVideoAI for faster draft passes, reference-image tests, lower-cost iteration, live specs, and pricing before moving selected outputs into Veo 3.1.

overview: Veo 3.1 Fast is the current faster Google Veo 3.1 variant for text-to-video drafts, image-to-video tests, 8s reference-to-video runs with 1-4 stills, short concept loops, and lower-cost iteration with optional native audio and live pay-per-second pricing.

hero.title: Veo 3.1 Fast — current Veo variant for faster drafts and iteration
hero.intro: Use Google Veo 3.1 Fast as the quicker Veo 3 AI workflow for text-to-video drafts, image-to-video tests, reference-to-video runs from 1-4 stills, short concept loops, and lower-cost iteration before moving selected outputs into Veo 3.1.
hero.badge: 720p/1080p · 4–8s · Text, references, start image, last frame & extend

secondaryLinks.label: Compare Veo 3.1 Fast vs Veo 3.1 Lite
Compare LTX 2.3 Fast vs Veo 3.1 Fast

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 4 s, 6 s, 8 s per render; Reference mode is 8 s

technicalOverview.label: Formats
technicalOverview.body: 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1; 1:1 is available on text runs and start+last-frame shots

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video; Image-to-Video starts from one image; Reference mode accepts 1-4 stills; First/Last frame uses one opening and one ending image; Extend from an existing video

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Optional native audio (VO, ambience, SFX) via per-job toggle

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: The pricing card uses MaxVideoAI scenario totals for duration, resolution and audio on/off. Specs use the same display pricing helper for audio-on and audio-off per-second rates; the exact quote appears before generation.

technicalOverview.label: Render timing
technicalOverview.body: Short queue times; live estimates shown in the jobs rail

promptStructure.title: Text and Reference-to-Video with Google Veo 3.1 Fast
promptStructure.quote: 8s 16:9 reference-to-video run using three campaign stills: preserve the same jacket, product color and rainy street mood while the camera tracks one step forward. Audio on: city ambience and one short VO line.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts tight, single-scene and sensory. Use one start image for Image-to-Video, attach 1-4 stills in Reference-to-Video for an 8s consistency pass, or use first/last frames when the ending composition is approved.
steps: Subject + focal point (one idea for the whole clip)
Light and mood (daylight, neon night, warm office, etc.)
Audio (optional): ambience + short VO, or say “no dialogue”
Format and duration (16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 when available; 4/6/8 s)
Reference-to-Video: attach 1-4 stills for an 8s consistency pass; name what each reference should preserve.

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: ⚡ Use Fast as a sandbox for A/B tests and prompt tweaks
🧩 Use one start image for Image-to-Video, or Reference mode with 1-4 stills when identity, wardrobe, or product continuity matters
🧷 Add a Last frame when the opening and ending frames are already approved
🎚️ Audio toggle: rough mix when on, cheaper runs when off
📱 Optimize for social formats where 4–8 seconds is the story
🖼️ Single-scene focus; avoid multi-location narratives in this tier
🔁 Clone winners to Veo 3.1 for final 1080p polish and richer audio

prompts.title: Misty forest slow push
prompts.prompt: Misty forest at dawn, slow push-in toward a mossy rock as light rays cut through trees, subtle birdsong, no dialogue, 8s, 16:9 at 1080p.

prompts.title: Reference product walk-through
prompts.prompt: 8s 16:9 reference-to-video shot using 1-4 product and wardrobe stills, slow tracking camera through a neon street, preserve face, outfit, logo shape, and material details, subtle city ambience.

prompts.title: Late-night desk draft
prompts.prompt: 8s 16:9 Veo 3.1 Fast draft of a presenter working late at a minimalist desk, city lights blurred through a large window. Medium close-up, slow handheld drift, cool window light plus warm desk lamp, soft typing and one short calm line.

faqs.q: Can I use Veo 3.1 Fast for text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference testing?
faqs.a: Yes. Veo 3.1 Fast supports quick text-to-video runs, Image-to-Video from one start image, and 8s Reference mode with 1-4 stills when you want to compare prompts, framing, identity, and timing before finaling in Veo 3.1.

faqs.q: When should I use Veo 3.1 Fast instead of Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Use Fast for exploration, social variants and internal reviews; switch to Veo 3.1 for final hero shots and paid media.

faqs.q: Does Veo 3.1 Fast support vertical videos?
faqs.a: Yes. Veo 3.1 Fast supports 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 at 720p or 1080p. Choose 9:16 for mobile-first placements and 1:1 for square social crops.

faqs.q: Can I reuse Veo 3.1 prompts in Fast?
faqs.a: Yes. Clone a Veo 3.1 render, change engine to Veo 3.1 Fast, keep framing/tone presets, and adjust length/resolution.

faqs.q: How does pricing compare to Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Use the pricing card for preset MaxVideoAI display totals: each scenario states duration, resolution and audio on/off. Specs use the same helper for per-second display rates, and Generate shows the exact live quote before you run variants.

faqs.q: Can I run Veo 3.1 Fast from Europe or LATAM?
faqs.a: Yes. It uses the same MaxVideoAI wallet/workspace and licensed DeepMind routing—no separate billing needed.

custom.heroDesc1: Veo 3.1 Fast brings DeepMind’s cinematic engine to a lower price point and faster turnaround, perfect for rapid testing, reference-image consistency checks, guided endings, and social loops.
custom.heroDesc2: Start from a short text brief, one start image for Image-to-Video, or 1-4 reference stills for an 8s consistency pass. Add a Last frame only when you need to steer the ending, or extend an existing clip, then choose orientation, resolution, audio, and generate variants fast enough to A/B multiple ideas in one session.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for exploring hooks, guided endings, and pacing fast before you regenerate winners in Veo 3.1.
heroHighlights: Optimized for speed + business workflows||Built for programmatic ad generation and rapid creative testing at scale.
Keeps Veo’s cinematic intent||Clear camera language + style cues translate well into consistent variants.
Sound-ready drafts||Great for rough timing and vibe; polish the final take in the premium tier.
Simple upgrade path||Draft in Fast, then regenerate the keepers in Veo 3.1 for higher-fidelity finals.
custom.whatTitle: What Veo 3.1 Fast Actually Is in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Veo 3.1 Fast is tuned for speed and efficiency within the Veo family.
custom.whatIntro2: You keep framing and tone presets, but runs are optimized for cost and turnaround.
whatFlowSteps: 1. Pick Veo 3.1 Fast as the engine.
2. Start from text, add one Start image for Image-to-Video, or attach 1-4 reference stills in Reference mode.
3. Use Reference mode for 8s identity/product consistency, or add a Last frame when you need to steer the ending.
5. Toggle audio on/off, then paste a tight, sensory prompt.
6. Check the live price and render.

custom.imageTitle: Image and Reference-to-Video Flow with Google Veo 3.1 Fast
custom.imageIntro: Animate one start image into a quick loop, or attach 1-4 reference stills for an 8s consistency pass.
imageFlow: Upload one lifestyle or product start image, or attach 1-4 reference stills in Reference mode.
Choose Image → Video for 4/6/8 s, or Reference mode for an 8s consistency pass.
Prompt subtle camera and subject motion; add light audio or keep it silent.
Generate variants by changing one element at a time (camera speed, VO tone, time of day).
imageWhy: Quick hero shots for landing pages or ecommerce
Animated social posts from brand photography
Mood loops behind titles or UI elements
custom.multishotIntro1: Use Fast to explore prompts cheaply, then move winners to Veo 3.1.
custom.multishotIntro2: Framing and tone presets are shared, so migration is smooth.
multishotTips: Render 2–4 variants in Fast with small prompt tweaks.
Promote the best to Veo 3.1 for higher fidelity and audio depth.
Stay single-scene in Fast; use other Veo tiers for multi-beat control.
strengths: Lead with framing + camera move (wide/medium/close + dolly/pan/handheld) before style.
Keep one hero subject and one readable action per clip.
Use 2–3 concrete anchors (time of day, weather, 1–2 set details) instead of long lists.
For continuity, reuse the same “shot recipe” (palette, lighting, lens feel) and change one variable per take.
Audio is best with minimal cues: ambience + 1 key sound, or one short VO line.
boundaries: Single-scene focus works best; multi-location narratives tend to drift.
This tier is draft-first (slightly less detail/fidelity than full Veo 3.1).
Tiny UI text and small lettering are unreliable — add in post.
safetyRules: No sexual content, and nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or graphic-violence content.
Don’t impersonate real people or public figures; use consent for any likeness/voice.
Don’t include private personal data (addresses, phone numbers, documents, non-consenting faces).
Some prompts or references may be blocked by provider safety filters.
custom.comparisonTitle: Veo 3.1 Fast vs Veo 3.1 – Quick Overview
comparisonPoints: Veo 3.1 Fast: cheaper, faster; best for tests, social loops, internal reviews.
Veo 3.1: higher fidelity and richer audio; best for client-ready and paid media.
Workflow: Prototype in Fast, then regenerate winners in Veo 3.1.

faqs.q: Can I use reference images with Veo 3.1 Fast?
faqs.a: Yes. Use one start image for Image-to-Video, or Reference mode with 1-4 stills for an 8s run when you need stronger identity, wardrobe, product, or style continuity.

faqs.q: When should I use Veo 3.1 Fast instead of Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Use Fast to explore ideas, social variants, and internal reviews; use Veo 3.1 for final hero shots and paid media.

faqs.q: Does Veo 3.1 Fast support vertical videos?
faqs.a: Yes—16:9 and 9:16 at 720p or 1080p; pick 9:16 for mobile-first placements.

faqs.q: Can I reuse Veo 3.1 prompts in Fast?
faqs.a: Yes. Clone a Veo 3.1 render, switch to Veo 3.1 Fast, keep framing/tone presets, tweak length/resolution.

faqs.a: Use the pricing card for preset MaxVideoAI display totals: each scenario states duration, resolution and audio on/off. Specs use the same helper for per-second display rates, and Generate shows the exact live quote before you run variants.

faqs.a: Yes. Same MaxVideoAI wallet/workspace and licensed DeepMind routing—no separate billing.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare price tiers, latency, and prompt presets across the rest of the catalog.
custom.finalPara1: Veo 3.1 Fast in MaxVideoAI is your rapid-fire test bench for Veo prompts.
custom.finalPara2: Same cinematic language, lower stakes, and quality that’s ready for social delivery.
custom.tipsIntro: Veo 3.1 Fast is most reliable when you write a short shot brief: one subject, one action, clear framing, and one camera move.
troubleshootingItems: Drift / ignores details → move subject + action + framing to the first line; remove extra beats; keep constraints positive (“clean background”, “centered subject”).
Motion feels messy → one camera move only; slow the action; simplify the background.
Off-brand look → lock palette + lighting; reuse the same wording; add a reference frame when you can.
Text/signage breaks → keep readable text off-screen; overlay critical copy in post.
VO / lip sync feels off → shorten lines; avoid fast speech and long monologues.

section.title: How to Write a Great Veo 3.1 Fast Prompt
section.intro: Veo Fast prefers short, concrete prompts and one clear action.
section.tip: Tip: duration + aspect ratio are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style, and sound cues.
section.referencesTitle: How Veo 3.1 Fast uses start, reference and ending frames

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want fast Veo variants.
tabs.copy: 8s 16:9 Veo 3.1 Fast draft of a product founder walking through a rainy neon street, slow handheld tracking move, blue storefront reflections, audio on with city ambience and one short calm line.

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (reliable draft)
tabs.description: Separate subject, action, camera and audio so the fast route stays readable.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
Product founder in a navy rain jacket, holding a compact camera bag.

Action:
Walks one step forward, looks toward the storefront, then pauses.

Camera:
Medium shot, slow handheld tracking move, 16:9, 8 seconds.

Look:
Rainy neon street, blue reflections, clean commercial grade.

Audio:
City ambience, soft footsteps, one short calm VO line.

tabs.label: Reference-to-video
tabs.title: Reference-to-video prompt (1-4 stills)
tabs.description: Use this when attached stills should preserve identity, product, wardrobe or style.
tabs.copy: Reference-to-video, 8s, 16:9, 1080p, audio on. Use reference 1 for face and jacket, reference 2 for the product color, reference 3 for the rainy street palette. Keep the same identity and materials while the subject takes one step forward. Slow push-in, subtle city ambience, no extra characters.

tabs.label: First/Last
tabs.title: First/last frame prompt
tabs.description: Use this when both opening and ending compositions are already approved.
tabs.copy: First/last-frame Veo 3.1 Fast draft, 6s, 9:16, audio off. Start on the approved product table frame, end on the approved close-up hero frame. Smooth push-in only, keep background layout and label position stable, no new props, no text changes.

tabNotes.body: Quick = fast T2V variants with audio on/off.
Structured = clearer subject, action, camera and sound cues.
Reference-to-video = 1-4 stills for identity, product or style continuity.
First/Last = use when both endpoint frames are already approved.

globalPrinciples: Call out framing and camera move.
Use 2-3 visual anchors, not long lists.
engineWhy: Fast favors simple beats and short prompts.
Use style anchors to keep the look stable.

demo.title: Demo: Veo 3.1 Fast desk draft
demo.prompt:
8s 16:9 Veo 3.1 Fast draft of a presenter working late at a minimalist desk, city lights blurred through a large window.
Camera: medium close-up with a slow handheld drift, staying on the face, hands and laptop.
Lighting: cool blue window light mixed with a warm desk lamp.
Audio: soft typing, low city hum and one short calm line.
notes: One clear subject and one environment.
Audio supports the mood without adding complexity.
